reasoning: The latest value of the 1-year expected inflation (as of Nov 2025) is 2.704. Looking at the data over the last year, the series exhibits significant month-to-month volatility (std around 0.17) with a slight upward linear trend over the 1-year window, but a recent downward momentum (falling from 2.80 in Sept to 2.73 in Oct and 2.70 in Nov). Given this mixed signal, the best estimate is a near-random walk with a slight mean reversion or drift dynamics. The FRED tool statistical analysis estimated a 58% chance of the linear trend placing the next reading above the current one. However, taking the recent downward momentum and mean reversion into account, probabilities are pulled closer to 0.50. Following the guideline that uncertainty must increase over time, probabilities trend towards 0.50 as the horizon extends (0.53 for 1-month horizon, 0.51 for the 3-month horizon).
